title: Microchilus cruciformis
taxonomy: Microchilus cruciformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of orchid, which is a large and diverse group of plants. More specifically, it is classified within the subfamily Apostasioideae, which is one of the most ancient lineages of orchids. The subfamily Apostasioideae is further divided into several genera, including Microchilus. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Microchilus cruciformis.
uses: Microchilus cruciformis is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in Central and South America. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues, such as fever, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
